name: System.Actor
comment:
# Actor

In the context of this package an Actor is a Component that is mapped to the System.

Currently there are three components defined;

  - The Worker Component
  - The Ui Component
  - The Layout Component

An Actor probably looks very familiar to you,
that is because it closely resembles the Elm Program definition.

It should be relativaly straight forwards to map you exisiting Elm component on top
of one of the existing Components.

Actors can communicate directly with other Actors only when it knows it's PID.

@docs Actor, toSystemActor

aliases.name: Actor
aliases.type: { init : ( System.Internal.PID.PID, Json.Decode.Value ) -> ( actorModel, msg ), update : model -> msg -> System.Internal.PID.PID -> ( actorModel, msg ), view : model -> System.Internal.PID.PID -> (System.Internal.PID.PID -> Maybe.Maybe output) -> output, subscriptions : model -> System.Internal.PID.PID -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, events : System.Internal.Event.Event -> System.Internal.PID.PID -> System.Event.EventHandler msg }

values.name: toSystemActor
values.comment: Apply your model over your Actor to create a SystemActor
values.type: System.Actor.Actor model actorModel output msg -> model -> System.Internal.SystemActor.SystemActor actorModel output msg

name: System.Browser
comment: This module helps you set up an System Program. # Elements Create an HTML element managed by Elm. @docs element # Applications Create an application that manages Url changes. When the application starts, init gets the initial Url. You can show different things depending on the Url! When someone clicks a link, like Home, it always goes through onUrlRequest. The resulting message goes to your update function, giving you a chance to save scroll position or persist data before changing the URL yourself with pushUrl or load. More info on this in the UrlRequest docs! When the URL changes, the new Url goes through onUrlChange. The resulting message goes to update where you can decide what to show next. Applications always use the Browser.Navigation module for precise control over Url changes. @docs application

values.name: application
values.comment: Create an Application managed by Elm through a System
values.type: { apply : actorModel -> System.Internal.SystemActor.SystemActor actorModel output (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg), factory : actorName -> ( System.Internal.PID.PID, Json.Decode.Value ) -> ( actorModel, System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg ), init : flags -> Url.Url -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> List.List (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg), view : List.List output -> List.List (Html.Html (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg)), onUrlRequest : Browser.UrlRequest -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg, onUrlChange : Url.Url -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg } -> System.Platform.Program flags address actorName actorModel wrappedMsg

values.comment: Create an HTML element managed by Elm through a System.
values.type: { apply : actorModel -> System.Internal.SystemActor.SystemActor actorModel output (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg), factory : actorName -> ( System.Internal.PID.PID, Json.Decode.Value ) -> ( actorModel, System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg ), init : flags -> List.List (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg), view : List.List output -> Html.Html (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg) } -> System.Platform.Program flags address actorName actorModel wrappedMsg

name: System.Component.Worker
comment:
# Worker

A Worker is a [headless](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Headless_software) Actor, it has no user interface.

This is great if you want to use an Actor as the “brain” for something else.

@docs Worker, toActor

aliases.name: Worker
aliases.comment: The Type of a Worker Component
aliases.type: { init : ( System.Process.PID, Json.Decode.Value ) -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), update : msgIn -> model -> ( model, List.List msgOut, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msgIn ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msgIn, events : System.Event.ComponentEventHandlers msgIn }

values.name: toActor
values.comment: Create an Actor from a Worker
values.type: System.Component.Worker.Worker model msgIn msgOut -> { wrapModel : model -> actorModel, wrapMsg : msgIn -> wrappedMsg, mapIn : wrappedMsg -> Maybe.Maybe msgIn, mapOut : System.Process.PID -> msgOut -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg } -> System.Actor.Actor model actorModel (Html.Html msg) (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg)

name: System.Event

aliases.name: ComponentEventHandlers
aliases.comment: Components need to supply the following event handlers
aliases.type: { onPIDNotFound : System.Internal.PID.PID -> System.Event.EventHandler msgIn, onKill : System.Event.EventHandler msgIn }

values.name: beforeDefault
values.comment: Before the default behaviour respond with a custom msgIn

values.name: custom
values.comment: Ignore the default behaviour and respond with a custom msgIn

values.name: default
values.comment: Let the System decide what to do

values.name: ignore
values.comment: Ignore the event

values.name: ignoreAll
values.comment: You can choose to ignore all events

values.name: systemDefault

name: System.Message

aliases.name: SystemMessage
aliases.comment: The type of the System Messages

values.name: batch
values.comment: Batch perform a list of messages
values.type: List.List (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg) -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

values.name: kill
values.comment:
Kill a process

This will trigger the Actors onKill event on which you can decide what to do with this Message.

There is a Default behaviour available that will remove the Process from the System.

values.name: noOperation
values.comment:
Don't do anythinge

This can be handy for instance when you want to spawn an Actor but don't do anything with it's resulting PID.

    spaw MyActorWorker (always noOperation)

values.name: populateAddress
values.comment:
Add a PID to the System's view

The System will render views in the order it receives it.
values.type: address -> System.Internal.PID.PID -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

values.name: populateView
values.comment:
Add a PID to the System's view

The System will render views in the order it receives it.

values.name: sendToAddress
values.comment: Send a message to an Address
values.type: address -> wrappedMsg -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

values.name: sendToPid
values.comment: Send a message to a PID.
values.type: System.Internal.PID.PID -> wrappedMsg -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

values.name: spawn
values.comment: Spawn an Actor
values.type: actorName -> (System.Internal.PID.PID -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg) -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

values.name: spawnWithFlags
values.comment: Spawn an Actor with given flags (as an encoded JSON Value)
values.type: Json.Encode.Value -> actorName -> (System.Internal.PID.PID -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg) -> System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg

aliases.name: Program
aliases.comment:
This will be the type of your program when you create it using this package.

Checkout out the `element` and `application` functions in the System.Browser module to find out how to create a System.Program.

_A [Program](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/core/latest/Platform#Program) describes an Elm program! How does it react to input? Does it show anything on screen? Etc._
aliases.type: Platform.Program flags (System.Internal.Model.Model address actorName actorModel) (System.Internal.Message.Message address actorName wrappedMsg)

aliases.name: PID
aliases.comment: Each Process has an unique identifier ([PID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Process_identifier)).

values.name: equals
values.comment: Check if two PID's are the same
